title: Convergent genetic pathways linking neuropsychiatric and ocular disorders in children
abstract: Clinical and epidemiological studies conducted in children have suggested a potential link between neuropsychiatric and ocular disorders. We investigated the overall and local genetic correlations and causal relationship between eight neuropsychiatric disorders and five ocular disorders based on large-scale GWAS cohorts from PGC and UK Biobank. We discovered significant positive genetic correlations between ASD and myopia/astigmatism; inverse genetic correlation and potential causal relationship between ADHD and myopia/astigmatism. 124 loci were found to be shared between psychiatric diseases and refractive errors. Enrichment analyses highlighted early neurodevelopmental processes as key shared genetic mechanisms.
